title: Derris moniliformis
taxonomy: Derris moniliformis is classified within the family Leguminosae, commonly known as the pea family. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide variety of legumes. The species is further classified as a member of the genus Derris, a group of tropical legumes native to Asia. Within the genus Derris, Derris moniliformis is considered a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and distribution. Its classification is further supported by its morphological and molecular features.
uses: Derris moniliformis, also known as the Derris bean, has been used in traditional medicine for its various benefits. The root of the plant is a source of rotenone, a compound used to treat certain types of worm infestations. In traditional medicine, it is also used to treat fever and rheumatism. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and itching. Its medicinal properties have been utilized in various cultures around the world for centuries.
benefits: Derris moniliformis, commonly known as Derris, has been found to have various health benefits. The plant contains rotenone, a compound with insecticidal properties, which can also act as a natural antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Traditional medicine uses Derris to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts are also believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and its interactions with other medications.
properties: Derris moniliformis, also known as Derris, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various therapeutic properties. It contains rotenone, a natural insecticide that has been used to treat certain types of parasites and infections. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its roots are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. constituents: Derris moniliformis is a plant species that is known to contain active constituents such as rotenone and tephrosin, which are primarily responsible for its insecticidal properties. These compounds are a type of natural insecticide and have been used for centuries in traditional medicine and as a pest control agent. Rotenone is a well-documented compound found in Derris moniliformis, and it works by disrupting the nervous system of insects, ultimately leading to their death. Tephrosin is another active constituent of the plant, although its specific mode of action is less well understood. The combination of rotenone and tephrosin in Derris moniliformis makes it a potent natural insecticide.
parts: Derris moniliformis is a plant known for its medicinal properties, primarily utilized for its roots. The roots of Derris moniliformis contain rotenone, a toxic compound that has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation. In some cultures, the roots are also believed to possess anti-parasitic properties, helping to eliminate worms and other intestinal parasites. However, it's essential to exercise caution when using Derris moniliformis, as its toxic compounds can be harmful in large quantities. The plant's medicinal properties have been studied, but more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and risks.
preparations: Derris moniliformis, also known as Derris or Toleño, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for various purposes. In some cultures, the plant's root is used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, which are believed to have medicinal properties. side_effects: Derris moniliformis, commonly known as the jumbo bean or poison bean, can be toxic and cause several side effects if ingested. The seeds of the plant contain rotenone, a toxic compound that can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea if ingested in large amounts. Rotenone can also cause headaches, dizziness, and abdominal pain in some individuals. In severe cases, ingestion of Derris moniliformis seeds can lead to seizures, coma, and even death. Long-term exposure to rotenone, even in small amounts, has been linked to neurological damage and increased risk of certain cancers.
intro: Derris moniliformis, also known as the 'rosa' or 'derris', is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Asia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The health benefits of Derris moniliformis include its potential as an antiparasitic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory agent, making it useful in treating various diseases such as malaria, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include rotenone, a natural insecticide, and other alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Derris moniliformis include tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
